title: For the Caregiver
scripture_text: Let us not grow weary of doing good, for in due season we will reap, if we do not give up.
reflection: You cannot pour from an empty cup. Rest is not abandoning them. Rest is what makes you able to stay.
body:
You are tired in a way that sleep does not fix. The kind of tired that lives in the bones. The kind of tired that comes from loving someone through a long, hard season.

You are not failing. You are doing the hardest, holiest work there is. But the holy work will hollow you out if you do not let yourself be filled again.

Take a break today. Not a long one. An hour. A walk. A coffee with a friend. A bath. A book. Something that is just for you, not for them.

The Lord, who watched over Israel in the wilderness, is watching over you. He sees the late nights. He sees the sacrifice. He is pleased. And He is saying, rest.
prayer: Lord, I am tired. Be my rest. And show me one small way to be kind to myself today. Amen.
